title: Presence of Antimicrobial Resistance Genes in Organomineral Fertilizer
author: Glauco Mariano Teixeira Junior
materials: QIAGEN® DNeasy® PowerSoil® Pro Figure 1. DNeasy PowerSoil Pro Kit.

Gently mix the contents of the tubes by stirring, then place the reaction tubes in the thermocycler, following specific settings for Tet and antibiotic-resistant genes listed in Table 1 and Table 2 . Figure 8. 0.2 mL strip tubes with PCR reaction being placed in the thermocycler. %s %s %s %s

steps.title: Begin by measuring 250 mg of organomineral fertilizer
steps.instruction: Begin by measuring 250 mg of organomineral fertilizer. Figure 2. Organomineral fertilizer formulations.

steps.title: Then, add the measured organomineral fertilizer...
steps.instruction: Then, add the measured organomineral fertilizer and 800 µL of Solution CD1 to the PowerBead Pro tubes . Figure 3. DNeasy PowerSoil Pro Kit Solutions.

steps.title: Vortex the sample in the Bead...
steps.instruction: Vortex the sample in the Bead Beater for 00:00:45 at max speed.

steps.title: Ensuring tubes are balanced, secured, and...
steps.instruction: Ensuring tubes are balanced, secured, and the bead beater’s lid is closed for safety. Figure 4. Bead Beater used to Vortex the sample.

steps.title: Centrifuge tubes at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tubes at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute .

steps.title: Ensuring tubes are balanced symmetrically in...
steps.instruction: Ensuring tubes are balanced symmetrically in the rotor to prevent damage. Use balancing weights or dummy tubes if processing fewer samples. Figure 5. Example of tubes in Centrifuge.

steps.title: Transfer 500 µL - 600 µL...
steps.instruction: Transfer 500 µL - 600 µL of supernatant to a new 2 mL Microcentrifuge Tube.

steps.title: Add 200 µL Solution CD2 and vortex for 00:00:05
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL Solution CD2 and vortex for 00:00:05 .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Transfer 700 µL supernatant to a new tube.

steps.title: Add 600 µL Solution CD3 , vortex for 00:00:05
steps.instruction: Add 600 µL Solution CD3 , vortex for 00:00:05 .

steps.title: Repeat go to step #11 for remaining lysate
steps.instruction: Repeat go to step #11 for remaining lysate.

steps.title: Add 500 µL Solution EA ,...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL Solution EA , centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute , and discard flow-through.

steps.title: Add 500 µL Solution C5 ,...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL Solution C5 , centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute , discard flow-through, and centrifuge again for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Elute DNA by adding 100 µL...
steps.instruction: Elute DNA by adding 100 µL Solution C6 to the filter and centrifuging at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute .

steps.title: Store DNA at -30 °C
steps.instruction: Store DNA at -30 °C . Figure 6. The DNA is stored in the freezer at -30 °C and labeled with a unique recognizable identifier.

steps.title: Prepare PCR reactions in 0.2 mL strip tubes
steps.instruction: Prepare PCR reactions in 0.2 mL strip tubes: Figure 7. Materials used to evaluate the presence of antibiotic resistance genes in organomineral fertilizers. oMaster Mix JumpStart Red Taq (P0982-800rxn): 12.5 µL oPCR Water: 5.5 µL oForward Primer (10 µM): 1 µL oReverse Primer (10 µM): 1 µL oDNA Sample: 5 µL (Negative control: PCR Water in place of DNA sample) (Positive control: gBlock variety in place of DNA sample)

steps.title: Add gBlock variety listed in Appendix...
steps.instruction: Add gBlock variety listed in Appendix A (Guidelines & Warnings Section), for Tet and antibiotic-resistant genes as follows: o tetA : 1 µL gBlock 12 + 4 µL PCR water o tetX : 2 µL gBlock 10 + 3 µL PCR water o tetO (gBlock 13), ermB ( gBlock 17), intI1 ( gBlock 19), bla CTX-M-32 (gBlock 18), sul 1 (gBlock 3): 3 µL gBlock + 2 µL PCR water

steps.title: Figure 9
steps.instruction: Figure 9. Gel casting tray, and gel comb. Prepare the agarose gel by dissolving 0.4 g of agarose and 50 mL of 1x TAE buffer in an Erlenmeyer Flask and mix gently to combine.

steps.title: Cover the opening of the Erlenmeyer...
steps.instruction: Cover the opening of the Erlenmeyer flask with a paper towel.

steps.title: Place the mixture in the microwave...
steps.instruction: Place the mixture in the microwave for 00:00:30 , until the solution begins to bubble.

steps.title: Check for clarity, and if necessary,...
steps.instruction: Check for clarity, and if necessary, repeat until the solution is clear.

steps.title: Be sure to use thermal gloves...
steps.instruction: Be sure to use thermal gloves when handling the bottle to prevent burns.

steps.title: Add 2 µL ethidium bromide (for...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL ethidium bromide (for tetA, tetX, tetO ), or 4 µL SYBR safe (for ermB , intI1 , bla CTX-M-32 , sul 1 ).

steps.title: After heating, carefully pour the mixture...
steps.instruction: After heating, carefully pour the mixture from the bottle into the gel casting tray, ensuring the gel comb is already in place.

steps.title: Allow the mixture to cool to...
steps.instruction: Allow the mixture to cool to Room temperature until it solidifies and develops a gelatinous texture. Figure 10. SYBR safe containers.

steps.title: Place the gel, along with the...
steps.instruction: Place the gel, along with the casting tray, into the Gel Electrophoresis Apparatus.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.25x TAE buffer to the tank, ensuring the gel is fully submerged and completely covered by the buffer.

steps.title: Load 10 µL of the PCR...
steps.instruction: Load 10 µL of the PCR amplicon into the gel wells ( 40 µl ).

steps.title: Run gel at 130 V for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Run gel at 130 V for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Analyze gel under UV light to...
steps.instruction: Analyze gel under UV light to detect DNA bands for positive results. CAUTION : UV light can damage your eyes. Eye protection is required. Figure 11. UV light analyzer and protective glasses used to analyze the gel.

steps.title: Transfer 650 µL lysate to MB Spin Column
steps.instruction: Transfer 650 µL lysate to MB Spin Column. Centrifuge at 15000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Discard flow-through.

steps.title: Turn on the scale, place the...
steps.instruction: Turn on the scale, place the tube on it, and tare to zero before carefully weighing the sample.

steps.title: Refer to the safety information for...
steps.instruction: Refer to the safety information for using ethidium bromide provided in Appendix A (In 'Warnings' section) for detailed guidance.

steps.title: See Appendix B (in 'Guidelines' section) for the results interpretations
steps.instruction: See Appendix B (in 'Guidelines' section) for the results interpretations.
